len(qdata), type(qdata[0])

# show a few entries
for i in range(0,5):

    print(qdata[i]['ayah_text'][:200])

target = "وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِي مَا عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلَابِيبٍ"

# normalize function
import unicodedata, regex as re
def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tashkeel (diacritics), tatweel, punctuation, unify hamza variants, lowercase, collapse spaces
    # but for matching we will create both diacritics-stripped and original
    # remove tashkeel
    tashkeel = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED]')
    text = re.sub(r'ـ', '', text)  # tatweel
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s\u0621-\u064A]', ' ', text)  # remove punctuation but keep arabic letters
    text = tashkeel.sub('', text)
    # unify hamza forms to bare hamza 'ء' maybe convert to 'ا'?? We'll unify alef variations to 'ا'
    text = text.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    # replace taa marbuta to heh? Maybe keep 'ة' as is.
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text.lower()

# search for jalaabeeb occurrences
target_word = "جَلَابِيب"
norm_target = normalize_ar(target_word)
occ = []
for d in qdata:
    if norm_target in normalize_ar(d['ayah_text']):
        occ.append(d)
len(occ)

needle = "وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ"
[n for n in qdata if normalize_ar(needle) in normalize_ar(n['ayah_text'])][:10]
[a for a in qdata if "وَلَيْسَ" in a['ayah_text']][:20]

# Search for normalized exact match within db entries for the substring
substr = "وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِي مَا عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلَابِيبٍ"
nsubstr = normalize_ar(substr)
candidates = []
for d in qdata:

        candidates.append(d)
len(candidates), candidates[:2]

import difflib
cand = occ[0]['ayah_text']
norm_cand = normalize_ar(cand)
norm_sub = normalize_ar("وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِي مَا عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلَابِيبٍ")
norm_cand, norm_sub, difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, norm_cand, norm_sub).ratio()

# compute ratio for every ayah vs the substring normalized
from tqdm import tqdm
best = []
for d in qdata:
    r = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, normalize_ar(d['ayah_text']), norm_sub).ratio()
    best.append((r, d))
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_sorted[:10]
